<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs=""
>
[Fulcrum Lenses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=942)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=81) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Fulcrum lenses_ are unique magical crystals that each contain a sliver of [Nhimbaloth's](/Deities.aspx?ID=163) essence. They belong to a larger set of lenses created to manipulate or even bind the Empty Death; most of the other lenses are long lost and likely destroyed. The Haruvex family came into possession of four of the lenses, and they knew that bringing them together focused Nhimbaloth's attention in unprecedented and dangerous ways. Belcorra brought all four lenses to the Abomination Vaults with her, intending to install them in Gauntlight for her ultimate revenge upon Absalom. She also created a special receptacle called the [_Fulcrum Lattice_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=943) to hold the lenses so that their power could be focused together. She realized the danger of keeping the _fulcrum lenses_ too close together until the right time and spread them out among loyal groups in the Abomination Vaults' lowest levels for safekeeping.

 Belcorra keeps only the most powerful lens, the _Ebon Fulcrum Lens_, with her—indeed, as a ghost, it's the only one she can carry. If the heroes can bring the other three lenses to bear against Belcorra, they can draw Nhimbaloth's dread glare to the sorcerer. Despite her loyalty, Belcorra can't hope to keep her spirit intact when Nhimbaloth gazes directly upon it.

 The _fulcrum lenses_ are more than mere receptacles of Nhimbaloth's essence. Each grants those who use it a fragment of the Great Old One's power.

You gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects and a +2 item bonus to your melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=89) damage (this increases to a +4 item bonus to damage if the melee Strike is a jaws attack). You can also activate the lens in the following ways. 

 **Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You grasp the Crimson Fulcrum Lens in one hand and make a Strike that doesn't require that hand. The Strike deals an additional 1d8 precision damage (or 2d8 precision damage if you make a jaws Strike) and doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty. 

 **Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129)); **Effect** While grasping the lens, make a melee spell attack roll with a modifier of +18. On a hit, you force the splinter of Nhimbaloth's essence from the lens to possess the target. You're no longer invested in the lens, and the target gains the benefits as though it had invested the lens but can't activate the lens's other abilities. This effect is permanent, but it can be ended by any effect that removes a possession effect. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs=""
>
[Fulcrum Lattice](/Equipment.aspx?ID=943)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Abomination Vaults</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=81) pg. 76

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This silver and copper framework is shaped to hold four focusing lenses, one behind the other, from smallest to largest. It has a socketed base to be mounted into a stand, such as that found in a lighthouse lantern. The _Fulcrum Lattice_ was specifically designed to hold the four fulcrum lenses from smallest to largest (ebon, crimson, ochre, emerald), and it hums slightly while within 10 feet of any fulcrum lens. A fulcrum lens slotted into the _Fulcrum Lattice_ has no Bulk; the lattice remains at 1 Bulk and can be carried easily. Inserting or removing a lens from the _Fulcrum Lattice_ requires a single Interact action.

 The Fulcrum Lattice channels the power of [fulcrum lenses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=942) joined with it. While you hold the _Fulcrum Lattice_, lenses socketed into it are considered to be in your hand for the purpose of activating their abilities. When you make a melee spell attack with a lens in the Fulcrum Lattice, you use the highest melee spell attack modifier of any lens in the lattice, or you can use your melee attack modifier with simple weapons if it's greater.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fulcrum Lattice

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix</spoilers>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small, opalescent stone glows with a light that constantly shifts between colors. When you activate the stone, you throw it into a space within 100 feet. The stone then casts [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) on you and transports you to itself. This destroys the stone.

 You can instead hurl the stone at a creature in an attempt to bring that creature closer to you. Make a ranged Strike against a creature using your highest proficiency with a ranged weapon. The swapping stone has a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 15 feet. On a hit, the stone shatters against the creature, casting _dimension door_ on the creature and causing it to instantly teleport to a space adjacent to you. An unwilling creature can resist this teleportation with a successful DC 28 Will save.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bonmuan Swapping Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=944)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small, opalescent stone glows with a light that constantly shifts between colors. When you activate the stone, you throw it into a space within 100 feet. The stone then casts [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) on you and transports you to itself. This destroys the stone.

 You can instead hurl the stone at a creature in an attempt to bring that creature closer to you. Make a ranged Strike against a creature using your highest proficiency with a ranged weapon. The swapping stone has a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 15 feet. On a hit, the stone shatters against the creature, casting _dimension door_ on the creature and causing it to instantly teleport to a space adjacent to you. An unwilling creature can resist this teleportation with a successful DC 28 Will save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Phoenix Fighting Fan](/Equipment.aspx?ID=945)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Fighting Fan](/Weapons.aspx?ID=93)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elegant _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) [fighting fan](/Weapons.aspx?ID=93)_ features sharp silver feathers instead of traditional paper leaves in its design.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call upon the fan's extradimensional powers to trap a creature in Hao Jin's repository, a small demiplane that holds the countless relics Hao Jin collected over her lifetime (though a trapped creature cannot interact with any of these relics). The fighting fan casts [_maze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=187) on a creature within 30 feet. The fan automatically Sustains the Spell as long as you Strike a creature with it each round. The [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) or Perception DC to escape the demiplane is 34. The spell ends once the creature escapes, you fail to Strike a creature with the fan during a round, or after 1 minute, whichever comes first.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (envision, Interact);     **Effect** The fan casts 8th-level [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=243), consuming the _phoenix fighting fan_ in the process.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phoenix Fighting Fan

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Phoenix Necklace](/Equipment.aspx?ID=946)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn necklace

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This brilliant jewelry is made from fine silver interwoven with a uniquely malleable form of ruby that causes the necklace to glimmer like a flickering fire. Hao Jin created the necklaces to mitigate the potential of death during the Ruby Phoenix Tournament pre-qualifier, and she specially attuned each _phoenix necklace_ to the silver feathers she created for the pre-qualifying round. While the silver feathers themselves aren't magical, they are required to activate the necklace's power. Each team starts with 3 feathers hidden in their headquarters; they acquire more through challenges and events. Additionally, the tournament emissary's enforcers have the ability to deliver messages to the necklace's wearer at any time by casting [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=272), though the wearer can't respond.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision;     **Effect** You select any number of willing creatures you can see within 30 feet, including yourself. The selected creatures can make [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks without taking a penalty to their attack rolls. This effect remains in place until you remove the necklace or use this activation again.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (envision, Interact);     **Effect** The necklace consumes one of the attached silver feathers and casts 7th-level [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=243). Activating this ability is particularly costly and every activation requires consuming one more silver feather than the previous activation.

**Destruction** Once a _phoenix necklace_ has 10 silver feathers attached, Hao Jin can transform the necklace into a [_phoenix fighting fan_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=945), a process which requires 3 Interact actions.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phoenix Necklace

[Apricot of Bestial Might](/Equipment.aspx?ID=947)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 74

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This yellow fruit's soft flesh is covered by waxy, fibrous leaves reminiscent of a pineapple. When you eat an apricot of bestial might, you transform into a boar-faced battle form with scaly skin. You can use feats with this item as if it were a [bestial mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=82), improving your tusk unarmed attack as if it were a claw or jaws attack.

 **Benefit** You gain 8 resistance to all physical damage and gain a tusk unarmed attack with the [deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174) trait that deals 1d10 piercing damage.

 **Drawback** You become [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3). If you perform an action with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) or [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) traits, you must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the action is lost.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Apricot of Bestial Might

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Cape of the Open Sky](/Equipment.aspx?ID=948)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 74

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This cloth of gold cape was originally gifted to one of Goka's emperors centuries ago. Since then, it has spawned many imitations, which are all alike in that they are dyed with an elaborate seal depicting two drakes flanking Goka's palace and the Seven Dragons Bridge. The beautiful cape grants you a +3 item bonus to checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) and to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50). While you wear the cape, the weather (including wind and fog) doesn't affect your movement or vision. Additionally, whenever you fall while wearing the _cape of the open sky_, the cape automatically casts [_feather fall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=111) on you.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** The cape becomes a pair of golden drake wings that grant you a fly Speed of 30 feet for 1 hour.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The cloak pushes you onward. You Stride or [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) up to your Speed, but you can move only in a straight line. You gain a +15-foot status bonus to your Speed and fly Speed for this movement.

---

These silk headbands always come in pairs and usually feature a prominent symbol of a nation or team. If both wearers Invest their headbands at the same time and think of the same symbol while doing so, both headbands change to display that symbol until they are removed. As long as you both have Invested your headbands, you can [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75) each other without taking an action to prepare and when you get a critical failure when attempting to Aid an ally with a paired headband, you get a failure instead.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You remove your headband, which teleports you to a space adjacent to the other Invested wearer's location, if you are within 1 mile of each other.

Large turquoise gems stud the outer edge of this sturdy _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)_. Arrows shot from the bow are unimpaired by wind and air effects.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156))
 **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You shoot an arrow at an open space on a surface within 500 feet and momentarily vanish into a wind that carries the arrow. You teleport to that location.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56))
 **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You shoot an arrow at the sky and it tears an extraplanar rift that resembles a storm cloud in a 30-foot burst centered on a point within 200 feet. A torrent of rain pours down from the rift for 1 minute or until you Dismiss the effect. The area within the torrent becomes difficult terrain for all movement (including [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=94)). As an Interact action, you can fire an arrow into the rift to cause a bolt of lightning to strike a creature in the area. The creature takes 5d12 lightning damage and must attempt a DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (command, envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You fire a volley of arrows into the sky. You create the effects of a successful [_control weather_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=9) ritual.

This paper tag is inscribed with magical symbols. When you activate the fulu, it vanishes in a wisp of cloud and you gain resistance 15 to cold, electricity, and sonic damage until the end of your next turn. For that duration, you ignore difficult terrain from wind and weather and you can't be forcibly moved or teleported unless the effect [counteracts](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the fulu (DC 40). These protections apply against the triggering effect.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs=""
>
[Brain Cylinder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=953)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Malevolence</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Malevolence](/Sources.aspx?ID=88) pg. 66

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _brain cylinder_ allows the extracted brain of a Large or smaller creature to continue to function even after it has been removed from the body. As long as the brain remains in the cylinder, the creature remains alive and can continue to think, even though its body is dead. Detachable modules that fit into the base of the cylinder allow the brain to see, hear, or speak using a raspy speaker. So long as these are attached, the brain can speak and understand any languages it knew in life, though nothing within the cylinder compels it to do so if it is unwilling. It also retains its living alignment, and can use Intelligence-, Wisdom-, and Charisma-based skills. A standard _brain cylinder_ has one skill at +15, one at +13, and two at +11, as chosen by the GM.

 The brain retains no other abilities it possessed in life, including purely mental ones. [Mi-go](/Languages.aspx?ID=67) typically preserve humanoid brains in this manner when they wish to interrogate a creature at a later date, or when they wish to preserve a creature as a resource for consultation or research purposes, as a brain preserved in this manner can live for centuries. If a _brain cylinder_ is destroyed, the brain dies and its soul finally moves on to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15). Restoring a brain in a _brain cylinder_ to its former body can be done by any effect that restores life (and, in most cases, any capable of creating a new body, since the original is typically missing or long gone).

 Note that the level 6 _brain cylinder_ presented above represents a level 6 creature's brain. A less powerful or more powerful brain can be placed in a _brain cylinder_, which can affect the brain cylinder's Perception, mental ability scores, Will save, and skill modifiers (and can drastically alter the value of the brain cylinder), but the physical properties of the _brain cylinder_ remain unchanged. Higher-level _brain cylinders_ can be crafted, with resistances and weaknesses increasing as appropriate to their level. However, they always have low AC and HP and terrible Fortitude and Reflex saves for a creature of the _brain cylinder's_ level (see the [Building Creatures](/Rules.aspx?ID=995) rules).

 You can open a _brain cylinder_ by taking three consecutive actions with the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. If the _brain cylinder_ contains a brain, the brain dies 3 rounds after it's removed or if the cylinder is left open for more than 10 minutes. You can transfer a brain from one _brain cylinder_ to another with a DC 25 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) (expert) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) (expert) check. This is a two-action activity with the manipulate trait. On a critical failure, the brain dies.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Brain Cylinder

May contain spoilers from Malevolence **Source** [Malevolence](/Sources.aspx?ID=88) pg. 67 **Price** 650 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- Even during the time of Thassilon, when the Order of the Starless Night still existed, _starless scopes_ were uncommon. Today, this final scope might well be the last one to survive the passage of time. A _starless scope_ is a handheld telescope crafted of silver and fine wood. When one peers at the sky through a _starless scope_, one always views the sky as if during a clear night. A starless scope adds a +2 item bonus to [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) and Perception checks made to observe the skies, as it causes all celestial objects to dim when not directly observed, allowing its user to more easily observe their intended subject. It also grants a +2 item bonus to all [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) checks made to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24). A starless scope also functions as a divine focus for worshipers of [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5). The magic of a starless scope requires a specially prepared lens to function, and it's possible to shatter the lens if you roll poorly when activating the _starless scope_. Without a functional lens, a _starless scope_ doesn't function. You can replace a shattered lens with a functional lens by taking a two-action activity with the manipulate trait. If you know the formula for the starless scope, you can [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) a new lens with a DC 25 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) (master) check. A starless scope lens is a level 9 item worth 100 gp. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** you use the _starless scope_ as a divine focus; **Requirements** you worship Desna; **Effect** You attempt to enhance the effects of a spell as you cast it by attempting a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) check against the spell's DC, as determined by [Table 10–5](/Rules.aspx?ID=554) **Critical Success** Any attack rolls you make with the spell gain a +2 status bonus, and any saving throw a foe makes against the spell takes a –2 status penalty. If you would normally cast the spell from a spell slot, and the spell is 5th level or lower, you don't spend the spell slot and cast the spell from the _starless scope_ instead.
**Success** Any attack rolls you make with the spell gain a +1 status bonus, and any saving throw a foe makes against the spell takes a –1 status penalty.
**Failure** The spell is cast normally. Attempt a DC 11 flat check. On a failure, the _starless scope's_ lens shatters.
**Critical Failure** The spell is disrupted and the _starless scope's_ lens shatters. **Activate** Interact ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You raise the scope to your eye and observe a creature within 30 feet. You can attempt to identify the creature by [Recalling Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26). The _starless scope_ adds a +2 item bonus to this check. If the creature is associated with the Dominion of the Black or the Elder Mythos, or is a creature whose actions have blasphemed against Desna (Ioseff Xarwin's ghost falls into this latter category), the creature must attempt a DC 27 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, and the _starless scope's_ lens shatters.
**Success** The creature shines with starlight for 1 round. During this time, the creature can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) if it is visible. If it's [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), it's concealed rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). The creature takes a –1 status penalty to saving throws against [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) effects while it shimmers with starlight.
**Failure** As success, but the creature also suffers an additional effect. An [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creature loses its incorporeal trait as long as it shines with starlight. Any other creature becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) as long as it shines with starlight.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the duration of the starlight is 1 minute, and the status penalty to saving throws against divine effects increases to –2.

May contain spoilers from Malevolence **Source** [Malevolence](/Sources.aspx?ID=88) pg. 68 **Usage** mounted in frame (see below) **Bulk** 3 --- The _Void Mirror_ was created on a distant planet by a now-extinct alien cult to aid in unlocking the secrets of the Dark Tapestry. It appears as a 5-foot-tall, 2-foot-wide mirror of dark glass. As long as it's unmounted in a frame, the _Void Mirror_ functions only as a mirror, save that it always reflects the sky as if it were night, regardless of the time of day. When the _Void Mirror_ is mounted in a frame, it instead serves as a window into space, displaying a star field in its glass rather than a reflection. This frame can be of any quality, but stouter frames help to prevent the frame's destruction and thus help to keep the _Void Mirror_ functional. The view presented by the _Void Mirror_ is any view of the night sky possible from its current physical location, regardless of time of day. Observations of the sky in this way add a +3 item bonus to [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or Perception checks made while using the mirror. The point of view can be shifted through hand gestures, causing the scene within the mirror to reorient in any direction desired. A character who peers into the starscape within the _Void Mirror_ must attempt a DC 41 Will save. On a failure, the character feels something looking back at them from the dark places between the stars. On a critical failure, this sensation causes the character to become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). The character is then temporarily immune to this effect for 24 hours. By placing the _Void Mirror_ in a specially-constructed frame (such as the one within the Cosmic Crypt in this adventure), it can halt the ongoing transformation of a creature that has performed the apotheosis activation. Building such a frame is beyond the skill of low-level characters, as it requires an artisan with legendary proficiency in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4). The _Void Mirror_ can be utilized in other ways, but before a character can activate these effects the occult methods to access these ritual-like effects must be [Researched](/Rules.aspx?ID=1205) separately. Since the specific methods for these activations vary slightly from person to person, these methods can't be taught; each person who seeks to activate the _Void Mirror_ in one of the following ways must complete their own research to unlock the artifact's secrets. Each requires a separate number of [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) checks to Research, as detailed below for each activation. **Activate** 1 hour (Intract); **Research** Accumulate 12 RP by making DC 20 Occultism (expert) checks when Researching (1 week per attempt); **Frequency** once per month; **Effect** The first activation ritual is known as “Speak to the Void” and allows the user to contact an intelligence in a distant part of the universe, provided the mirror shows that location (regardless of distance). This alien intelligence infuses the user's mind with answers, allowing the user to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) using [Nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=22) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) as if they were legendary proficiency with the skill (including the +8 proficiency bonus). If this check fails, the character takes 10d6 mental damage (100 mental damage on a critical failure) as the imparted knowledge does more harm than good. **Activate** 1 hour (Interact); **Research** Accumulate 12 RP by making DC 30 Occultism (master) checks when Researching (1 month per attempt); **Frequency** once per year; **Effect** The second activation ritual is known as “Call from the Void.” The user performs a 1-hour-long rite before the _Void Mirror_, during which they indicate a type of [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20), [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=77), [ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=122), or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creature that's an alien life form from the planet the user is currently located on. The target creature must be a level equal to or less than the user's level +4. As the rite ends, the user attempts an Occultism check against the target creature's Will DC. **Critical Success** The creature is drawn across the universe to appear in a space adjacent to the _Void Mirror_. The creature's initial attitude toward the user is [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=21), but this attitude can shift quickly if the creature realizes its goals and personality differs dramatically from the user's, or if the user makes a request of the creature that's against its nature. As long both the creature and the user are within 30 feet of the _Void Mirror_, the user can return the creature to its original location by Dismissing this activation. The creature can attempt a Will save against the user's Occultism DC to resist being returned; if it succeeds, it's temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** As critical success, but the creature is [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=25) upon arrival.
**Failure** As critical success, but the creature is [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23) upon arrival.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but magical feedback from the failed activation causes the user to become [stunned 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). **Activate** 7 days (Interact); **Research** Accumulate 12 RP by making DC 40 Occultism (legendary) checks when Researching (1 year per attempt); **Frequency** once per century; **Effect** The third activation ritual is known as “Become the Void” and requires the user to continue performing the rite for 7 consecutive days (as if they were performing a multi-day ritual). If the user fails to perform this rite for one of these 7 consecutive days, they immediately suffer the critical failure effect below. **Critical Success** The user's body, mind, and soul are infused with alien energies drawn from the Dark Tapestry, which transforms the user into an [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1) of a level no higher than their current level +4. This new form could be an existing aberration or a unique one of the GM's creation. The user retains their memories and spellcasting ability (including any rituals they know) but becomes chaotic evil and loses all other abilities they possessed in their previous form.
**Success** As critical success, but the aberration the user transforms into can be of a level no higher than half the user's current level.
**Failure** The user's attempt to change is a painful mistake that deals 10d6+40 bludgeoning damage as their body twists, bends and breaks. Ultimately, they are not transformed.
**Critical Failure** The user is slain, but their body shifts and transforms into a brand new creature as the bodiless alien intelligence Tchekuth incubates within the user's flesh, mind and soul. This transformation takes another 7 days to complete but can be halted indefinitely if the _Void Mirror_ is placed in a specially constructed frame aimed at the body's location (regardless of any solid object between the mirror and the body). Slowing or stopping this transformation via other means requires complex rituals or divine intervention, subject to the GM's whim. After 7 days, the body completes its transformation and Tchekuth rises once more as a physical threat. **Destruction** A creature that completes the apotheosis of Becoming the Void can destroy the _Void Mirror_ by smashing it, though doing so also destroys the creature performing this action in a violent burst of occult energy
name: Void Mirror

    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Genealogy Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=956)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 34

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The genealogy mask may be purchased to strengthen your character’s story and roleplay, but cannot be used to impact gameplay during Organized Play adventures._

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Alijae ethnicity

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These large masks are forged of precious metals or carved of wood and tasked with guarding the history of a family. Most Alijae are granted a _genealogy_ mask when they come of age, which they bequeath to their next of kin upon death. When a member of the family recounted in the _genealogy mask_ performs a particularly significant event while wearing the mask, its eyes and ears animate, allowing the item to witness and record the deeds for posterity. Different families have different standards for their _genealogy masks_—some may value accuracy and objective, clinical facts, while others may relish embellishing their stories to the very brink of believability in the name of a good story.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You ask the mask about the deeds of a particular ancestor, and the mask speaks for 10 minutes, recalling the tales it knows about that ancestor. The mask is limited by what information a particular ancestor shared with it. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Genealogy Mask

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />

<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This enormous stone glows with a pale light and emits a quiet hum. Each _Jahan waystone_ is part of a paired link. The paired stones correspond in the color of the light they emit, as well as the specific tone at which they hum.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact);     **Effect** You place your hand on the stone and focus on the stone's light. The stone's glow envelopes all creatures in a 10-foot radius with its light and attempts to teleport the creatures to its paired stone. If there is no open space within 30 feet of the target waystone, the teleportation attempt fails. A creature can resist the teleportation with a successful DC 45 Will save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Jahan Waystone

[Oath of the Devoted](/Equipment.aspx?ID=958)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You gain fire and mental resistance 5. When you die, you rise as an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature with the [zombie](/Traits.aspx?ID=245) trait on the next round; if you are a PC, you become an NPC under [Walkena's](/Deities.aspx?ID=159) control. Your stats remain the same, except that your alignment changes to evil. If you reach 0 Hit Points as a zombie, you are destroyed and do not rise again.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain a +1 status bonus to Will saves. Choose a weapon or an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strike; your chosen attack deals an extra 1d6 fire damage for the next 1 minute. 

**Special** If you are a zombie at a time when Mzali is entirely purged of foreigners, your duty is fulfilled and you are immediately destroyed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Oath of the Devoted

Originally designed by a fanatical sect of [Gozren](/Deities.aspx?ID=8) priests, _goz masks_ were designed to help navigate the area around the Eye of Abendego. The masks couldn't contend with the might of the storm, but they remain in use by people all around the Mwangi Expanse. These masks are typically made of wood and crafted to sport round, exaggerated features.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You ignore [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) caused by fog, smoke, and other obscuring vapors for 1 minute.

**Price** 210 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

All _herd masks_ are linked to at least one other _herd mask_ and are usually sold in sets of multiple masks. Linked _herd masks_ look similar to one another, with only the slightest of differences to tell them apart.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You swap locations with another creature wearing a linked herd mask within 100 feet. If you and the creature you swapped with are disguised as each other, other creatures gain an immediate Perception check against the lower of your or the other wearer's [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DCs to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) each other. On a failure, they don't realize the swap occurred.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69). You can link a _herd mask_ you create to another _herd mask_ in your possession when you finish crafting it, which causes it to be linked to the chosen mask as well as all other _herd masks_ that are also linked to the chosen mask.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Herd Mask

This simple wooden mask is carved into the shape of an animal, usually tailored to a species of beast that the user favors. While wearing this mask, you can speak even when [polymorphed](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) into an animal. The _shapespeak mask_ does not otherwise remove any limitations, such as the inability to cast spells when transformed.

---

This carved wooden full-head mask sports several holes along each side of the face. The first time each day that you begin your turn [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) and within 25 feet of an enemy, metallic insect legs emerge from the holes in the mask and Step 5 feet away from the nearest enemy, dragging your body along with the mask. If more than one enemy is equidistant, the mask Steps away from one of them at random. The mask possesses no special senses and does not react to [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) enemies, nor can it distinguish that a creature not acting openly hostile is an enemy.

>
[Tlil Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=968)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #169: Kindled Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=94) pg. 75, [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 155

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Colorful, beaded _tlil masks_ are most commonly found on the distant continent of Arcadia, but trade between the two regions means that they can be found in the Mwangi Expanse as curiosities. These masks usually bear floral patterns and attune your senses to plants of all varieties.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your vision up to 60 feet sees through small amounts of living plant matter as though it were transparent. While this effect is active, creatures can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from you due to living plants, such as small trees, vines, and grass. This also prevents them from [Hiding](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneaking](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) past you using only living plants for concealment or cover. Other than the inability to use the cover to Hide or Sneak, this ability doesn't prevent plants from providing cover to creatures or blocking line of effect. It also doesn't allow you to see through dead plant matter, such as the wooden walls of a building, or thick plant matter, such as the walls of a dungeon built entirely inside an enormous living tree. The effect lasts for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tlil Mask

</row>

---

Used to write secret messages, a single dose of forgetful ink is enough to pen a page of text, often with flourishes extending to the page's edges. Anyone handling the page is exposed to the poison. This includes casual reading, unless the reader specifically takes precautions not to physically touch it. Unlike other poisons, forgetful ink retains its potency for one year, regardless of the number of victims exposed.

**Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude ([secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142)); **Onset** 1 minute; **Stage 1** The reader forgets the contents of the writing, recalling it as blank or as inconsequential text.

</column>

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #169: Kindled Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=94) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small, enchanted bone (most often a jaw) is usually capped with metal at its ends. It's activated by striking it against a hard surface.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** The bone begins recording the next 1 minute of ambient sounds (including speech) that require a DC 15 or lower Perception check to hear. The bone's recording doesn't copy magical [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) effects, such as those of spellcasting, a [harpy's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=258) song, or a [banshee's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=39) wail. Once the _rhythm bone_ has been activated in this way, it retains the recorded sound for the rest of its existence and can't be activated this way again.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** The bone replays its recorded sounds.

**Craft Requirements** supply a casting of [_ghost sound_](/Spells.aspx?ID=132).

</column>

This unassuming yellow paper, affixed above a door or gate, flickers in the light. Pick an alignment trait: [chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99), or [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64). When a creature with the opposing alignment trait of the fulu (good for an evil fulu, and so on) passes through the entrance, it must succeed at a DC 17 Fortitude save or become [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34); regardless of whether they succeed, the creature becomes temporarily immune for 1 day.

</column>

Given to undisciplined acolytes at risk of breaking their vows, this fulu activates on its own if its wearer begins to perform an act anathema to a specific deity or cause, decided at the time of crafting. The fulu heats up when activated, giving you enough warning to correct your actions; if you proceed, the fulu immolates in a burst of flame. This deals 4d6 fire damage and brands your skin until you receive an appropriate [_atone_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2) ritual. Either way, activating the fulu consumes it.

</column>

</column>

---

This fulu comes in four pieces, one placed in each cardinal direction. Choose one of the following traits when Activating the fulus: [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20), [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70), [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=111), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). Those within the fulus' circle upon activation (including the fulus themselves) gain the effect of [_invisibility sphere_](/Spells.aspx?ID=165), but only against creatures with the chosen trait. If any of the fulus are moved or destroyed after activation, the effect ends.

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<row gap="medium">

**Trigger** You attempt a Fortitude save against an [ingested](/Traits.aspx?ID=95) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126).

 
</row>

</column>

---

The monkey on this fulu sits on the point of the armor just over the bearer's stomach, happily drinking any intoxicants that come flowing down. When you activate the fulu, you gain a +2 status bonus on the triggering Fortitude save and on Fortitude saves against the same poison for the next minute. If the triggering poison was normal alcohol, you instead automatically succeed at the triggering save and gain a +4 status bonus on saves against alcohol for 1 minute.

The silver ink on this black paper charm ebbs in and out of sight, especially when caught out of the corner of your eye. Usually placed in the kitchen, this fulu slows the spread of fire in a 30-foot radius by half.

 If the fulu itself catches fire, as it burns, it releases a shower of water in a 30-foot cone, automatically putting out non-magical fires in this area and attempting to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any magical [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) effects with a +7 counteract modifier and a counteract level of 2; when this happens, the fulu's duration ends early.

>
[Fulu of Flood Suppression](/Equipment.aspx?ID=976)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 159

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 week

**Usage** affixed to a wall

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This blue fulu bears dark ink that slowly swirls and twists about itself, expanding and contracting on the paper over the course of the day in time with the tides. This fulu is most effective in the lowest area of a building, or near the most valuable or least-waterproof items. They're often seen as the rainy season approaches, with demand spiking just before a particularly large storm hits.

 The fulu slows the spread of flooding water in a 30-foot radius by half, repelling the remaining water away. If the fulu becomes submersed, as the ink dissolves in the water, it releases a rush of heat in a 10-foot emanation, harmlessly vaporizing non-magical water in this area into steam and attempting to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any magical [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) effects with a +7 counteract modifier and a counteract level of 2; when this happens, the fulu's duration ends early.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fulu of Flood Suppression

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stormbreaker Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=984)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 161, [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 75

**Price** 1,900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You would be forced to move, you would be teleported, or you would take [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) damage.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This unassuming paper tag is inscribed with magical symbols. When you activate the fulu, it vanishes in a wisp of cloud, and you gain resistance 15 to cold, electricity, and sonic damage until the end of your next turn. For that duration, you ignore difficult terrain from wind and weather, and you can't be [forcibly moved](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or teleported unless the effect [counteracts](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the fulu (DC 40). These protections apply against the triggering effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stormbreaker Fulu

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tracking Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=985)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 161

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to creature or object

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row><actions string="Free Action" /> envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Used covertly by assassins and spies, this pair of fulus stick to one another when created but can be easily separated. Affix one fulu to a target before activating its pair. When activated, the unaffixed fulu flutters toward the affixed one at a speed of 30 feet per round, traveling for up to 1 hour and fluttering more rapidly the closer it comes to its pair. The unaffixed fulu always moves in a straight line towards the affixed fulu; it can't solve mazes or find its way through winding paths.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tracking Fulu

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fu Water](/Equipment.aspx?ID=987)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 161

**Price** 21 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Burnt fulu ashes float in this liquid, lending a distinctive red color and imparting it with a variety of purifying effects. Unlike many [fulu](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=61) items, _fu water_ must be created using [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) magic.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** This potion functions as a bottle of [_holy water_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=245). Like holy water, you throw it as a Strike. It's a simple [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon with a range increment of 20 feet. Unlike an alchemical bomb, it doesn't add the [manipulate](/Actions.aspx?ID=639) trait to the attack made with it.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You drink the _fu water_ to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) ailments within yourself. Attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with a +9 counteract modifier and a counteract level of 3 against one effect that imposes the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) condition. You are then [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fu Water

This pamphlet-sized book records the various symbols seen on fulu and also contains some ready-made magic on a special page that restores itself every day at sunrise. Using the compendium as a reference grants a +2 item bonus to any skill checks to determine a fulu's function or authenticity.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You rip a page from the _fulu compendium_ and cast it in a wide arc; as it flies in that arc, it multiplies into a storm of fulus. All undead creatures in a 30-foot emanation are affected by a [_spirit-sealing fulu_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=983) with a save DC of 25.

</column>

</title>

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 162

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A female figure peers through her blindfold from the cover of this stately tome.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** Your last action was to cast a [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) spell prepared from this grimoire; **Effect** The grimoire offers you a glimpse into the truth of things. [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26).

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Endless Grimoire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=992)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 162

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When opened, this grimoire has unlimited pages that, oddly, seem almost eager to transcribe spells. Unlike most grimoires, it has no limits to its number of spells. The grimoire's eagerness to contain your spells grants you a +1 item bonus to checks to [Learn a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=25) if you do so by transcribing the spell into the grimoire. If you use the grimoire during your daily preparations and are capable of preparing spells of the appropriate level, the grimoire's nature leaks into your mind, allowing you to prepare an additional 1st-level spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Endless Grimoire
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 162

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Endless Grimoire (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 162

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and the additional spell is 3rd level.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Endless Grimoire (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 162

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and the additional spell is 5th level.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Endless Grimoire (True)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 162

**Price** 23,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, and the additional spell is 7th level.
name: Endless Grimoire

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Instructions for Lasting Agony](/Equipment.aspx?ID=993)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 163

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This worn and stained manual emits a chill when opened.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a harmful [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) spell that you prepared from this grimoire and that allows a saving throw, you warp and twist negative energy into the spell to cause intense pain. If the target fails its saving throw against the spell, it becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) by the pain.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Spellbook of Redundant Enchantment](/Equipment.aspx?ID=994)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 163

**Price** 420 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When opening the book, whispers can be heard on the wind, or laughter rings in the distance.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You cast an [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) spell prepared from this grimoire that has no effect because all targets critically succeeded on their saving throws;  **Effect** You quickly divert the failed enchantment energy into yourself to retain your favored spell in place of another. You lose another spell you prepared of an equal or higher level to the triggering enchantment spell but retain the ability to cast the enchantment spell again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

---

This green spellbook with gold trim contains a cautionary story about a boy who was eaten by a wolf after he previously lied about wolf attacks. When opened, the engravings on the front seem to move in a loop, enacting this story.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature succeeds, but doesn't critically succeed, at a Perception check to disbelieve an [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) spell you prepared from this grimoire;  **Effect** You quickly tell a fib to try to smooth over the inconsistencies in your illusion. Attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against the triggering creature's Perception DC. If you succeed, the creature doesn't disbelieve the illusion.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />

**Bulk** L

---

This grimoire takes the form of a series of baked clay tablets. Any text inked upon it turns swiftly into carved depressions.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a beneficial [abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2) spell on yourself or a single ally, you use the tablets' power to infuse the warding with additional abjurations and attempt to remove a harmful effect. Your spell attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) a harmful spell effect of your choice on the target. This isn't without risks, however. If your attempt fails to remove the harmful effect, the warding energy is consumed by the unyielding malediction, and you lose the abjuration spell's normal effects. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Familiar Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=998)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 164

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo typically consists of an image of a small animal or of a familiar's name written in runes. Your familiar can meld into your _familiar tattoo_ to be carried in your skin. The familiar must spend a single action, which has the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114), and [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) traits, to meld into or exit your tattoo. It must be adjacent to you to meld into your tattoo, and it exits your tattoo in an open space adjacent to you. Any of the familiar's companion items remain on it, but it can't carry any other items with it. This tattoo is non-magical if you don't have a familiar or if your familiar doesn't remain present for the entire tattooing process. If your familiar dies, this tattoo loses its magic and becomes a mundane tattoo.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Familiar Tattoo

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Rune of Sin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=999)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 165

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This jagged glyph, one of the [Thassilonian runes of sin](/Rules.aspx?ID=1567), reacts to magic of a particular school (there's no rune for [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), as it was considered lesser within sin magic). When you Cast a Spell of the school matching the sin, you gain resistance 5 to damage from spells until the start of your next turn. This resistance is increased to 7 against spells of the matching school. This tattoo has the school trait matching the rune: [abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2) for envy, [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) for gluttony, [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) for greed, [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) for lust, [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) for pride, [conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33) for sloth, and [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65) for wrath.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rune of Sin

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Warding Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1000)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 165

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Many cultures of Golarion have a tattoo to turn away harm; as such, warding tattoos often resemble whichever dangers are most common to the culture, such as a wild beast or a whirlpool in the high seas of the Shackles.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An enemy, hazard, or the environment makes an attack against your AC, requires you to attempt a saving throw, or causes you to take damage automatically;  **Effect** Until the end of the current turn, against the triggering effect, you gain a +1 status bonus to AC and saving throws and gain resistance 2 to damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Warding Tattoo
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 165

**Price** 140 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Warding Tattoo (Trail)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 165

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 2 to damage from [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=454) and [environmental](/Traits.aspx?ID=62) hazards at all times, which increases to 5 when the tattoo is activated.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Warding Tattoo (Wave)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 165

**Price** 220 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

In addition to the normal triggers, you can activate the tattoo when you enter an environment where you can't breathe. When you activate the tattoo, it casts [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) on you, in addition to the other effects.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Warding Tattoo (Fiend)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 165

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain resistance 2 to spells cast by [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) and magical attacks by fiends, which increases to 5 when the tattoo is activated.
name: Warding Tattoo

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Warding Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1000)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 165

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Many cultures of Golarion have a tattoo to turn away harm; as such, warding tattoos often resemble whichever dangers are most common to the culture, such as a wild beast or a whirlpool in the high seas of the Shackles.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An enemy, hazard, or the environment makes an attack against your AC, requires you to attempt a saving throw, or causes you to take damage automatically;  **Effect** Until the end of the current turn, against the triggering effect, you gain a +1 status bonus to AC and saving throws and gain resistance 2 to damage.

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 165 **Price** 6,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This staff was created as an example of the [personal staves rules](/Rules.aspx?ID=1499). Moss and winding vines give this gnarled staff of wild wood a vibrant green tinge. You created this staff to aid you in speaking to the plants you met on your adventure and beseeching them to come to your aid. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.
  • **Cantrip** [_tanglefoot_](/Spells.aspx?ID=330)
  • **1st** [protector tree](/Spells.aspx?ID=976), [_shillelagh_](/Spells.aspx?ID=282)
  • **2nd** [_entangle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=103), [_shape wood_](/Spells.aspx?ID=277)
  • **3rd** [_tree shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=342), [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=366)
  • **4th** [_barkskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=20), [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294)
  • **5th** [_plant form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=223), _wall of thorns_
  • **6th** [_nature's reprisal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=954), _plant form_
**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells.
name: Lini's Leafstick

    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dazzling Rosary](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1003)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 168

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When energy courses through these lustrous beads, they glow brightly in the sacred colors of the spellcaster's faith. A [_spiritual weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=306) empowered with this catalyst flashes with bright light when it critically Strikes a target, causing the target to be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the beginning of your next turn. The type of rosary determines the maximum spell level of _spiritual weapon_ that can use the rosary as a catalyst.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dazzling Rosary
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 168

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The rosary can be used with a _spiritual weapon_ of 4th level or below.

level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dazzling Rosary (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

name: Dazzling Rosary

When energy courses through these lustrous beads, they glow brightly in the sacred colors of the spellcaster's faith. A [_spiritual weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=306) empowered with this catalyst flashes with bright light when it critically Strikes a target, causing the target to be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the beginning of your next turn. The type of rosary determines the maximum spell level of _spiritual weapon_ that can use the rosary as a catalyst.

</column>

</row>

</column>

---

[Shelynites](/Deities.aspx?ID=17) originally crafted this complex knot of vibrant string for bracelets meant to tie the destinies of two people together. Now, however, spellcasters of all religions use them to enhance their capacity with teleportation magic. Adding this catalyst to a 4th-level [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) spell allows you to bring a single willing adjacent creature along with you; however, the teleportation is somewhat disorienting for them, causing them to become [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dimensional Knot

These compacted pellets of bat guano, sulfur, and magical accelerants have long been a staple for spellcasters on the battlefield. Adding a _firestarter pellet_ to a [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) spell produces clinging flames that deal [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to all who fail the saving throw against the effect (doubling on a critical failure).

Light refracts in strange ways through these precisely ground glass tiles, lengthening the refracted force and causing it to push back. Adding this catalyst to a [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=363) spell increases the wall's maximum length to 80 feet and maximum height to 40 feet and causes creatures that try to move into the wall's space (or are [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) into the wall) to take 2d6 [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Force Tiles

[Noxious Incense](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1008)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sold only in single sticks, this foul incense comes coated with a bevy of alchemical smoke that activates in the presence of intense heat, releasing its namesake pungent odor. Adding this catalyst to a [_wall of fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=362) spell fills all squares adjacent to the wall with thick, foul smoke. Creatures in this area are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and other creatures are concealed to creatures in the area. The smoke lasts for the duration of the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Noxious Incense
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 169

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The incense can be used with a _wall of fire_ of 6th level or below.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Noxious Incense (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 169

**Price** 750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The incense can be used with a _wall of fire_ of any spell level.
name: Noxious Incense

This luminous mica dust fluoresces for a short time after being exposed to significant amounts of magical energy. When a creature fails its save against a cloud of [_glitterdust_](/Spells.aspx?ID=136) created using this catalyst, glowing grains stick to them, causing them to shed dim light in a 20-foot radius for as long as their invisibility is negated by the spell as well as causing them to take a –2 circumstance penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) for that duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shimmering Dust

Performers popularized these bundles of aromatic herbs to calm the mind and ward off misfortune before a big show, though soldier bards have found them especially useful for clearing fear during particularly tumultuous battles. Adding this catalyst to a [_soothe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=291) spell also causes the spell to reduce the target's [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition value by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Soothing Scents

This carved chunk of amber contains a single tuft of gray feathers, which spark with electricity to create an odd jittery sensation in anyone holding the amber for long. When used as part of a [_shocking grasp_](/Spells.aspx?ID=283) spell, _thunderbird tuft_ funnels electricity back into the spellcaster in a defensive nimbus. For 1 minute, any creature that touches you or that hits you with a melee unarmed attack or non-[reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) melee weapon attack takes the listed electricity damage.

    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Waterproofing Wax](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1012)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 169

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Many books and spellbooks are treated in this wax formula to protect them from the elements, but _waterproofing wax's_ liquid-repellent properties can be further applied to split up a [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=140) spell into useful smaller sections. When the spell is cast in an area while using this catalyst, the conjured grease fills three 5-foot squares within 30 feet instead of its normal area; these squares don't need to be contiguous.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Waterproofing Wax

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 170 **Usage** affixed to armor or a weapon --- Identical feathers radiate from the center of this spellheart, held in place by woven straw. The spell attack roll of any spell cast by Activating this item is +8, and the spell DC is 18.
  • **Armor** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks and resistance 2 to falling damage.
  • **Weapon** After you cast an [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) spell by Activating the wreath, you can Fly 5 feet as a single action, or you can Fly 10 feet as a free action if the spell you cast wasn't a cantrip. This must be your next action, and if you don't have a fly Speed, you must end the flight on solid ground or fall.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_gale blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=917). Five-Feather Wreath **Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 170 **Price** 90 gp Five-Feather Wreath (Greater) **Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 170 **Price** 425 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 5, fly distance affixed to a weapon casting a non-cantrip spell is 25 feet, the spell attack roll is +14, and spell DC is 24. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_wall of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=367). Five-Feather Wreath (Major) **Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 170 **Price** 1,750 gp --- Bonus affixed to armor is +2, resistance when affixed to armor is 10, fly distance affixed to a weapon casting a non-cantrip spell is 40 feet, spell attack roll is +19, and spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-level [_wall of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=367). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6).
name: Five-Feather Wreath
